mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns following periods of sustained RSAFS growth are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and potential Fed tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures, which typically supports equity valuations due to lower real rates, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within 0.5%) in more than 40% of triggered instances over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, positive surprises in INDPRO data signal robust economic activity, leading to short-term positive market reactions as investors anticipate higher corporate profits over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% during an economic expansion regime.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer and business confidence, which typically precedes broader economic slowdowns and negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a sustained drop in HOUST below 800k units.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy labor market and strong economic activity, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and 20-day forward returns is consistently negative or zero (Rank IC < 0.01) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals aggressive monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs, slows economic growth, and negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances following a 25bp or more FEDFUNDS hike.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but industrial production (INDPRO) is lagging, it suggests a consumption-driven economy with potential supply-side constraints, which can lead to inflationary pressures and eventually dampen equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 3% AND INDPRO 3-month average growth < 0.5%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) during periods where GDPC1 growth outpaces INDPRO growth significantly.
